Thought 3: I need to evaluate the alibis. Clyde's alibi is directly contradicted by the neighbor who saw him in Faith's kitchen at the time of the murder. He claims he was mowing his lawn, but the witness places him at the crime scene. Paul's alibi is that he was at Faith's house "just before" the murder. This is vague and doesn't necessarily place him there at the exact time of the crime. Furthermore, Paul's motive is financial, while Clyde's is personal revenge. The narrative emphasizes Clyde's humiliation and his direct threat to "get even," which is a very strong and immediate motive.
Action 3: Do[Compare the strength of the motives and the credibility of the alibis]
